codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested minted-supply, first-vault, modeled whale-product, referral, and product-line revenue surfaces, but it still ships a blocking revenue-model conflict inside the tokenomics section and a smaller maturity-label inconsistency on Social Vaults.

issues.description: The fee-stack surface still tells users that social vault profits take a 10% performance fee, but the newly added product-family model says social vaults use a 20% fee and cites BETTER docs for that value (see src/content/token-tiers.ts:595 and :602). That leaves the tokenomics section with contradictory user-facing revenue math for the same product line, so the requested per-product revenue/value-return modeling is not internally correct.

The new modeled whale ladder labels Social Vaults as planned because ModeledWhaleProduct.maturity only allows planned/speculative (see line 404), even though the roadmap and the new social-vault revenue line both mark Social Vaults as in progress (src/content/roadmap-nodes.ts:76 and src/content/token-tiers.ts:593). The modeled framing is clear, but the maturity framing is now inconsistent across surfaces.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The content-strategy-worker skill says to write failing tests first, but this session's transcript shows implementation edits landed before the new token-policy regression suite was created while the handoff still reported followedProcedure=true. Either the skill expectations or the handoff-reporting standard need tighter enforcement/clarification for content features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 6c778f29-45a1-49da-a01e-e210eac9db01 shows Edit calls against src/content/token-tiers.ts before Create src/components/tokenomics/__tests__/token-policy-recalibration.test.tsx; .factory/skills/content-strategy-worker/SKILL.md step 3 requires 'Write failing tests first'; the handoff file records skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Fail. The new token-policy surfaces are mostly in place, but the tokenomics section still disagrees with itself on the social-vault performance-fee number (10% vs 20%), and the modeled whale ladder downgrades Social Vaults to Planned even though other surfaces keep it In Progress.
